news.headline: Crows hold off Cats in thriller for stunning win
news.body: The Crows have overcome the Cats in an enthralling clash.
news.ai_summary: Jordan Dawson (Adelaide) was best afield with 22 disposals and 3 goals in a captain's knock, making him a hold/POD target despite the modest possession count. Young gun Dan Curtin impressed with workrate and a game-saving mark, while recruit Callum Ah Chee tagged Tom Stewart to just 15 disposals and 3 intercepts before Stewart left with a hamstring injury — a major concern for Stewart owners. Adelaide also lost Jordon Butts and Ah Chee to injury, while Taylor Walker may face MRO scrutiny for a shove on Connor O'Sullivan. Ben Keays and debutant Hugo Hall-Kahan (head clash with Toby Murray) are also worth monitoring.

news.headline: Coach defends doctors as Crows players play out game after head clash
news.body: Matthew Nicks defends his medical staff after two Crows were cleared to play out their win against the Cats without a concussion test after a nasty head clash.
news.ai_summary: Adelaide's Callum Ah Chee (hamstring – his fourth since joining the Crows; 11 disposals, 1 goal) and Jordon Butts (calf) both failed to finish the one-point win over Geelong, with the extent unknown – owners should brace for outs. Debutant Hugo Hall-Kahan and Toby Murray passed concussion checks after a head clash and returned to play. Taylor Walker (2 goals on return) faces MRO scrutiny for shoving Connor O'Sullivan, risking a suspension. Skipper Jordan Dawson was the standout with 22 disposals and 3 goals despite a sore hip, while Patrick Dangerfield posted a season-high 30 disposals and 7 clearances in the loss.

news.headline: Crows, Cats lock in final teams for huge clash. LIVE from 7pm ACST
news.body: Follow all the action from Thursday night's clash between Adelaide and Geelong.
news.ai_summary: Geelong have recalled Jack Bowes, Jack Martin and Ollie Wiltshire for the Thursday night clash with Adelaide, with Lawson Humphries (ankle) out, Mitch Edwards managed and Brad Close dropped — denting the job security of Cats-owned mid-pricers. Adelaide bring back Taylor Walker and hand a debut to mid-season recruit Hugo Hall-Kahan, while Izak Rankine (calf) and Riley Thilthorpe (suspension) are notable fantasy outs. Rankine owners face a hit to scoring with no late changes available, while Hall-Kahan looms as a cheap cash-cow option for those chasing rookie value.

news.headline: F.O'Sullivan OUT: North Melbourne — Concussion
news.body: F.O'Sullivan has been ruled OUT for North Melbourne in this round. Reason: concussion.
news.ai_summary: North Melbourne youngster Finn O'Sullivan is OUT for Round 13 after entering concussion protocols, ruling him unavailable against Fremantle and creating a likely donut risk for Fantasy coaches holding him as a rookie. Zane Fisher comes in as his direct replacement. Fremantle, meanwhile, regains Hayden Young from concussion alongside Oscar McDonald and Jaeger O'Meara, a boost for Young owners after his recent absences. Trade urgency applies if O'Sullivan is on your field with no loophole option available.

news.headline: B.Cox OUT: Fremantle — Knee
news.body: B.Cox has been ruled OUT for Fremantle in this round. Reason: knee.
news.ai_summary: Fremantle's Brennan Cox is OUT with a knee injury for Saturday's clash with North Melbourne, alongside Matthew Johnson (hamstring), opening the door for Oscar McDonald, Hayden Young (returning from concussion) and Josh O'Meara. Young's return is the key fantasy-relevant inclusion for Dockers owners. North Melbourne lose Finn O'Sullivan (concussion), with Zane Fisher coming in. Cox owners will need to trade or bench him given the indefinite knee setback.

news.headline: M.Johnson OUT: Fremantle — Hamstring
news.body: M.Johnson has been ruled OUT for Fremantle in this round. Reason: hamstring.
news.ai_summary: Fremantle's Michael Johnson is OUT with a hamstring injury for the round 13 clash against North Melbourne, forcing coaches to trade or bench him. The Dockers regain Hayden Young (concussion) and also bring in Oliver McDonald and Jaeger O'Meara, with Bailey Cox (knee) also sidelined. North Melbourne lose young gun Finn O'Sullivan to concussion protocols, with Zane Fisher coming in. Hamstring setbacks make Johnson a hold-or-trade call depending on your bench cover.

news.headline: A.May OUT: Essendon — Shoulder
news.body: A.May has been ruled OUT for Essendon in this round. Reason: shoulder.
news.ai_summary: Essendon defender Aaron May is OUT for Sunday night's clash against Carlton with a shoulder injury, forcing coaches holding him to find a replacement or accept a donut. Sam Durham and Mason Redman return for the Bombers, while Darcy Parish (knee soreness) is also sidelined — a blow for owners given his midfield ceiling. Other key fantasy-relevant outs include Brisbane's Hugh McCluggage (calf) and Port Adelaide's Kane Farrell (adductor), while Fremantle welcomes back Hayden Young from concussion and Sydney regains Callum Mills and Brodie Grundy.
